Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Implement a function `read_feature_flag` that reads an environment variable specified by a key. It should parse the variable's string value as a boolean, treating "true", "1", or "yes" as `true`, and "false", "0", or "no" as `false`. If the environment variable is not set or contains an unrecognized value, the function should return a provided default boolean.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FeatureToggle
  (extern defun env_read ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")

  (defun read_feature_flag ((key : Str) (default_val : Bool)) : Bool
    (def raw_val : Str (env_read key))
    (if (or (str.eq raw_val "true") (or (str.eq raw_val "1") (str.eq raw_val "yes")))
      (return true)
      (if (or (str.eq raw_val "false") (or (str.eq raw_val "0") (str.eq raw_val "no")))
        (return false)
        (return default_val))))

  (test enabled_explicit_true
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_A" "true"))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_A" false) true))

  (test enabled_numeric_one
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_B" "1"))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_B" false) true))

  (test disabled_explicit_false
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_C" "false"))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_C" true) false))

  (test disabled_numeric_zero
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_D" "0"))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_D" true) false))

  (test missing_with_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_E" ""))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_E" true) true))

  (test missing_with_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_F" ""))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_F" false) false))

  (test unrecognized_value_with_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "FLAG_G" "invalid_value"))
    (assert-eq (read_feature_flag "FLAG_G" false) false)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Implement a function that counts the number of odd integers within a specified numerical range (inclusive). The function should handle cases where the start of the range is greater than the end.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module OddEvenCounter
  (defun count_odd_in_range ((start : Num) (end : Num)) : Num
    (if (> start end) (return 0) 0) ; If start is greater than end, return 0 (empty range)

    (def odd_count : Num 0)
    (def current_num : Num start)

    (while (<= current_num end) (do
      ; Check if the current number is odd.
      ; math.mod N 2 returns 1 for odd positive N, 0 for even N.
      ; For negative numbers, math.mod behavior can vary. Assuming non-negative range for simplicity based on common problem contexts.
      ; If 0 is passed to math.mod, it returns 0.
      (if (= (math.mod current_num 2) 1)
        (def odd_count : Num (+ odd_count 1))
        0) ; Do nothing if even

      (def current_num : Num (+ current_num 1))))

    (return odd_count))

  (test range_1_to_5 (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 1 5) 3)) ; Odds: 1, 3, 5
  (test range_2_to_6 (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 2 6) 2)) ; Odds: 3, 5
  (test single_odd (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 7 7) 1))   ; Odds: 7
  (test single_even (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 8 8) 0))   ; Odds: none
  (test empty_range_desc (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 5 1) 0)) ; Start > End
  (test range_0_to_0 (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 0 0) 0)) ; 0 is even
  (test range_10_to_10 (assert-eq (count_odd_in_range 10 10) 0))
)

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Implement a function that reads an environment variable specified by a key, interprets its string value as a boolean (e.g., "true", "false", "1", "0"), and returns a boolean. If the environment variable is not set, empty, or contains an unrecognised value, it should return a provided default boolean value.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FeatureFlag
  (extern defun env_read ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")

  (defun to_bool_strict ((s : Str) (default : Bool)) : Bool
    (if (str.eq s "true") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "True") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "TRUE") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "1") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "false") (return false) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "False") (return false) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "FALSE") (return false) 0)
    (if (str.eq s "0") (return false) 0)
    (return default))

  (defun get_feature_flag ((flag_name : Str) (default_value : Bool)) : Bool
    (def env_val : Str (env_read flag_name))
    (return (to_bool_strict env_val default_value)))

  (test feature_enabled_true
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ALPHA" "true"))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_ALPHA" false) true))

  (test feature_enabled_1
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_BETA" "1"))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_BETA" false) true))

  (test feature_disabled_false
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_GAMMA" "false"))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_GAMMA" true) false))

  (test feature_disabled_0
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_DELTA" "0"))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_DELTA" true) false))

  (test feature_missing_default_false
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_EPSILON" ""))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_EPSILON" false) false))

  (test feature_missing_default_true
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ZETA" ""))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_ZETA" true) true))

  (test feature_invalid_value
    (mocks (env.get "ENABLE_ETA" "maybe"))
    (assert-eq (get_feature_flag "ENABLE_ETA" false) false))
)

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Create a function that reads an environment variable and attempts to convert its value to an integer. If the environment variable is not set (empty string) or contains the specific non-numeric string "N/A", the function should return a provided default integer value. Otherwise, it should parse and return the integer.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module EnvIntReader
  (extern defun env_get_str ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")

  (defun get_env_int_or_default ((key : Str) (default_val : Num)) : Num
    (def raw_val : Str (env_get_str key))
    (if (str.eq raw_val "")
      (return default_val)
      (if (str.eq raw_val "N/A")
        (return default_val)
        (return (str.to_num raw_val)))))

  (test default_on_empty
    (mocks (env.get "PORT" ""))
    (assert-eq (get_env_int_or_default "PORT" 8080) 8080))

  (test default_on_na
    (mocks (env.get "TIMEOUT" "N/A"))
    (assert-eq (get_env_int_or_default "TIMEOUT" 3000) 3000))

  (test valid_positive_int
    (mocks (env.get "MAX_CONNECTIONS" "100"))
    (assert-eq (get_env_int_or_default "MAX_CONNECTIONS" 10) 100))

  (test valid_negative_int
    (mocks (env.get "OFFSET" "-5"))
    (assert-eq (get_env_int_or_default "OFFSET" 0) -5))

  (test valid_zero
    (mocks (env.get "RETRY_COUNT" "0"))
    (assert-eq (get_env_int_or_default "RETRY_COUNT" 5) 0)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Retrieve the values of two specified environment variables and concatenate them into a single string. If an environment variable is not set, its value should be treated as an empty string.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module EnvVarConcatenator
  (extern defun env_read ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")

  (defun concatenate_env_vars ((key1 : Str) (key2 : Str)) : Str
    (def val1 : Str (env_read key1))
    (def val2 : Str (env_read key2))
    (return (str.concat val1 val2)))

  (test both_present
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "PREFIX" "User_")
      ("env.get" "NAME" "Alice"))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "PREFIX" "NAME") "User_Alice"))

  (test first_empty
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "PREFIX" "")
      ("env.get" "NAME" "Bob"))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "PREFIX" "NAME") "Bob"))

  (test second_empty
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "PREFIX" "Item-")
      ("env.get" "ID" ""))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "PREFIX" "ID") "Item-"))

  (test both_empty
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "VAR_A" "")
      ("env.get" "VAR_B" ""))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "VAR_A" "VAR_B") ""))

  (test specific_order
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FIRST" "Hello")
      ("env.get" "SECOND" "World"))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "FIRST" "SECOND") "HelloWorld"))

  (test reverse_order
    (mocks
      ("env.get" "FIRST" "Hello")
      ("env.get" "SECOND" "World"))
    (assert-eq (concatenate_env_vars "SECOND" "FIRST") "WorldHello")))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Calculate the total cost of a gym membership, including a one-time joining fee that may be subject to a percentage discount, and a recurring monthly fee for a specified number of months.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module GymMembership
  (defun calculate_discounted_joining_fee ((base_fee : Num) (discount_pct : Num)) : Num
    (return (- base_fee (/ (* base_fee discount_pct) 100))))

  (defun calculate_total_membership_cost ((monthly_rate : Num) (base_joining_fee : Num) (months : Num) (discount_pct : Num)) : Num
    (def discounted_fee : Num (calculate_discounted_joining_fee base_joining_fee discount_pct))
    (def total_monthly_cost : Num (* monthly_rate months))
    (return (+ discounted_fee total_monthly_cost)))

  (test no_discount_one_month
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 1 0) 150))

  (test no_discount_multiple_months
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 3 0) 250))

  (test twenty_percent_discount_six_months
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 50 100 6 20) 380))

  (test fifty_percent_discount_one_month_different_fees
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 75 150 1 50) 150))

  (test zero_months_only_joining_fee_with_discount
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 40 80 0 25) 60))

  (test full_discount_long_term
    (assert-eq (calculate_total_membership_cost 60 200 12 100) 720)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Calculate an employee's sales commission based on a tiered structure. Sales up to $1000 earn 5%, sales between $1001 and $5000 earn 7%, and sales above $5000 earn 10%. The commission is calculated incrementally for each tier.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SalesCommissionCalculator
  (defun round2 ((x : Num)) : Num
    (return (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100)))

  (defun calculate_tiered_commission ((total_sales : Num)) : Num
    (def current_commission : Num 0.0)
    (def remaining_sales : Num total_sales)

    ;; Tier 1: Up to 1000 at 5%
    (def tier1_limit : Num 1000.0)
    (def tier1_sales_eligible : Num (math.min remaining_sales tier1_limit))
    (def current_commission : Num (+ current_commission (* tier1_sales_eligible 0.05)))
    (def remaining_sales : Num (- remaining_sales tier1_sales_eligible))

    ;; Tier 2: From 1001 to 5000 (a range of 4000) at 7%
    (def tier2_range_limit : Num 4000.0)
    (if (> remaining_sales 0.0)
      (do
        (def tier2_sales_eligible : Num (math.min remaining_sales tier2_range_limit))
        (def current_commission : Num (+ current_commission (* tier2_sales_eligible 0.07)))
        (def remaining_sales : Num (- remaining_sales tier2_sales_eligible)))
      (do))

    ;; Tier 3: Above 5000 at 10%
    (if (> remaining_sales 0.0)
      (do
        (def tier3_sales_eligible : Num remaining_sales)
        (def current_commission : Num (+ current_commission (* tier3_sales_eligible 0.10))))
      (do))

    (return (round2 current_commission)))

  (test sales_0 (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 0.0) 0.0 0.01))
  (test sales_500 (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 500.0) 25.0 0.01))
  (test sales_1000_exact (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 1000.0) 50.0 0.01))
  (test sales_1500 (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 1500.0) 85.0 0.01))
  (test sales_5000_exact (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 5000.0) 330.0 0.01))
  (test sales_6000 (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 6000.0) 430.0 0.01))
  (test sales_10000 (assert-near (calculate_tiered_commission 10000.0) 830.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Develop a set of functions to calculate the final total of a shopping cart. This involves calculating the cost of individual items after their respective discounts, summing them to a subtotal, applying a global cart-level discount, then adding sales tax, and finally rounding the total to two decimal places.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ShoppingCartProcessor

  ;; Calculates the net cost of a single item line after applying its specific discount.
  (defun calculate_item_subtotal ((unit_price : Num) (quantity : Num) (item_discount_pct : Num)) : Num
    (def gross_cost : Num (* unit_price quantity))
    (def discount_amount : Num (/ (* gross_cost item_discount_pct) 100))
    (return (- gross_cost discount_amount)))

  ;; Applies a percentage discount to the entire cart's current total.
  (defun apply_global_discount ((current_total : Num) (global_discount_pct : Num)) : Num
    (def discount_amount : Num (/ (* current_total global_discount_pct) 100))
    (return (- current_total discount_amount)))

  ;; Adds sales tax to the current total based on a given tax rate percentage.
  (defun apply_sales_tax ((current_total : Num) (tax_rate_pct : Num)) : Num
    (return (* current_total (+ 1 (/ tax_rate_pct 100)))))

  ;; Rounds a numerical amount to two decimal places, suitable for currency.
  (defun round_to_two_decimals ((amount : Num)) : Num
    (return (/ (math.floor (+ (* amount 100) 0.5)) 100)))

  ;; Orchestrates the full shopping cart total calculation for two items.
  ;; It calculates item costs, sums them, applies a global discount, then tax, and finally rounds.
  (defun calculate_cart_total
    (
      (unit1 : Num) (qty1 : Num) (disc1 : Num)
      (unit2 : Num) (qty2 : Num) (disc2 : Num)
      (global_discount_pct : Num) (tax_rate_pct : Num)
    ) : Num
    (def item1_cost : Num (calculate_item_subtotal unit1 qty1 disc1))
    (def item2_cost : Num (calculate_item_subtotal unit2 qty2 disc2))
    (def combined_subtotal : Num (+ item1_cost item2_cost))
    (def after_global_discount : Num (apply_global_discount combined_subtotal global_discount_pct))
    (def after_tax : Num (apply_sales_tax after_global_discount tax_rate_pct))
    (return (round_to_two_decimals after_tax)))

  (test cart_no_discounts_no_tax
    (assert-eq
      (calculate_cart_total 10 2 0 5 1 0 0 0)
      25.00)) ; (10*2) + (5*1) = 20 + 5 = 25. No discounts, no tax.

  (test cart_item_discounts_with_tax
    (assert-eq
      (calculate_cart_total 10 2 10 5 2 20 0 10)
      28.60)) ; Item1: (10*2)*0.9 = 18. Item2: (5*2)*0.8 = 8. Subtotal = 26. No global disc. Tax: 26 * 1.1 = 28.6.

  (test cart_all_discounts_with_tax
    (assert-eq
      (calculate_cart_total 10 1 0 5 2 10 5 8)
      19.49)) ; Item1: (10*1)*1 = 10. Item2: (5*2)*0.9 = 9. Subtotal = 19. Global disc: 19 * 0.95 = 18.05. Tax: 18.05 * 1.08 = 19.494. Rounded: 19.49.

  (test cart_complex_values_and_rounding
    (assert-eq
      (calculate_cart_total 1.99 3 5 12.50 1 0 12 7.5)
      17.19)) ; Item1: (1.99*3)*0.95 = 5.6715. Item2: (12.50*1)*1 = 12.50. Subtotal = 18.1715. Global disc: 18.1715 * 0.88 = 15.99092. Tax: 15.99092 * 1.075 = 17.189249. Rounded: 17.19.
)

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Create a function that reads an environment variable named "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" and interprets its string value as a boolean. It should return `true` for "true", "1", or "yes" (case-insensitive), and `false` for "false", "0", "no" (case-insensitive). If the variable is not set or contains any other value, it should default to `false`.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FeatureToggle
  (extern defun env_read ((key : Str)) : Str @capability "env.get")

  (defun parse_bool_str ((s : Str) (default_val : Bool)) : Bool
    (def lower_s : Str (str.lower s))
    (if (str.eq lower_s "true") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq lower_s "1") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq lower_s "yes") (return true) 0)
    (if (str.eq lower_s "false") (return false) 0)
    (if (str.eq lower_s "0") (return false) 0)
    (if (str.eq lower_s "no") (return false) 0)
    (return default_val))

  (defun is_feature_x_enabled () : Bool
    (def env_val : Str (env_read "FEATURE_X_ENABLED"))
    (return (parse_bool_str env_val false)))

  (test enabled_true_lowercase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "true"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) true))

  (test enabled_true_uppercase
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "TRUE"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) true))

  (test enabled_one
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "1"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) true))

  (test enabled_yes
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "yes"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) true))

  (test disabled_false
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "false"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) false))

  (test disabled_zero
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "0"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) false))

  (test default_empty_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" ""))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) false))

  (test default_invalid_string
    (mocks (env.get "FEATURE_X_ENABLED" "foobar"))
    (assert-eq (is_feature_x_enabled) false)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Create a program that reads a file and returns only its first line. If the file contains no newline characters, the entire file content should be considered the first line.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FirstFileLine
  (extern defun file_read ((path : Str)) : Str @capability "file.read")

  (defun get_first_line ((path : Str)) : Str
    (def content : Str (file_read path))
    (def newline_index : Num (str.index_of content "\n"))
    (if (= newline_index -1)
      (return content)
      (return (str.substring content 0 newline_index))))

  (test empty_file
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/empty.txt" ""))
    (assert-eq (get_first_line "/path/to/empty.txt") ""))

  (test single_line_no_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/single.txt" "Hello World"))
    (assert-eq (get_first_line "/path/to/single.txt") "Hello World"))

  (test single_line_with_newline
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/oneline.txt" "First line!\n"))
    (assert-eq (get_first_line "/path/to/oneline.txt") "First line!"))

  (test multiple_lines
    (mocks (file.read "/path/to/multi.txt" "Line A\nLine B\nLine C"))
    (assert-eq (get_first_line "/path/to/multi.txt") "Line A"))

    (def sum_val : Num 0)
    (def current_n : Num n)
    (while (> current_n 0) (do
      (def sum_val : Num (+ sum_val (math.mod current_n 10)))
      (def current_n : Num (math.floor (/ current_n 10)))))
    (return sum_val))

      (return 0)))

(module PrimeCounter
  (defun is_prime ((n : Num)) : Num
    (if (< n 2) (return 0) 0)
    (if (= n 2) (return 1) 0)
    (if (= (math.mod n 2) 0) (return 0) 0)
    (def i : Num 3)
    (def bound : Num (math.floor (math.sqrt n)))
    (while (<= i bound) (do
      (if (= (math.mod n i) 0) (return 0) 0)
      (def i : Num (+ i 2))))

        0)
      (def current_num : Num (+ current_num 1))))
    (return prime_count))

    (return (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100)))

  (defun get_zone_base_cost ((zone : Str)) : Num
    (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_A")
      (return 5.0)
      (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_B")
        (return 7.5)
        (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_C")

  (defun get_zone_rate_per_kg ((zone : Str)) : Num
    (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_A")
      (return 2.0)
      (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_B")
        (return 3.5)
        (if (str.eq zone "ZONE_C")
          (return 4.0)
          ; Fallback for unknown zone.
          (return 0.0)))))

Write a module that provides a function `count_primes_up_to` which calculates the total number of prime numbers less than or equal to a given non-negative integer `n`. It should utilize a helper function, `is_prime`, to determine the primality of each number.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module PrimeCounter
  (defun is_prime ((n : Num)) : Num
    (if (< n 2) (return 0) 0) ; 0 and 1 are not prime
    (if (= n 2) (return 1) 0) ; 2 is the only even prime
    (if (= (math.mod n 2) 0) (return 0) 0) ; Other even numbers are not prime

    (def i : Num 3)
    (def bound : Num (math.floor (math.sqrt n)))
    (while (<= i bound) (do
      (if (= (math.mod n i) 0) (return 0) 0) ; Found a divisor, not prime
      (def i : Num (+ i 2)))) ; Check only odd divisors
    (return 1)) ; No divisors found, it's prime

  (defun count_primes_up_to ((limit : Num)) : Num
    (def count : Num 0)
    (def current : Num 0)
    (while (<= current limit) (do
      (if (= (is_prime current) 1)
        (def count : Num (+ count 1))
        0) ; Do nothing if not prime
      (def current : Num (+ current 1))))
    (return count))

  (test count_zero (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 0) 0))
  (test count_one (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 1) 0))
  (test count_two (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 2) 1)) ; Prime: 2
  (test count_ten (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 10) 4)) ; Primes: 2, 3, 5, 7
  (test count_twenty (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 20) 8)) ; Primes: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19
  (test count_one_hundred (assert-eq (count_primes_up_to 100) 25)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write a function `trim_whitespace` that takes a string and removes all leading and trailing whitespace characters (space, tab, newline, carriage return). The function should return the modified string. Internal whitespace should remain untouched.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module TrimWhitespace
  ;; Helper function to check if a single character string is whitespace.
  (defun is_whitespace ((c : Str)) : Bool
    (or (str.eq c " ")
        (or (str.eq c "\t")
            (or (str.eq c "\n")
                (str.eq c "\r")))))

  ;; Removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.
  (defun trim_whitespace ((s : Str)) : Str
    (def len : Num (str.length s))
    (if (= len 0) (return ""))

    ;; Find the first non-whitespace character
    (def start_idx : Num 0)
    (while (and (< start_idx len)
                (is_whitespace (str.substring s start_idx 1))) (do
      (def start_idx : Num (+ start_idx 1))))

    ;; If all characters were whitespace or the string was empty, return empty string
    (if (= start_idx len) (return ""))

    ;; Find the last non-whitespace character
    (def end_idx : Num (- len 1))
    (while (and (>= end_idx start_idx) ; Ensure end_idx doesn't go below start_idx
                (is_whitespace (str.substring s end_idx 1))) (do
      (def end_idx : Num (- end_idx 1))))

    ;; Extract the substring from start_idx to end_idx (inclusive)
    ;; substring takes start index and length. Length is (end_idx - start_idx + 1)
    (return (str.substring s start_idx (+ (- end_idx start_idx) 1))))

  (test empty_string (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "") ""))
  (test no_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "hello") "hello"))
  (test leading_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "  hello") "hello"))
  (test trailing_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "hello  ") "hello"))
  (test both_leading_trailing (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "  hello  ") "hello"))
  (test internal_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "  hello world  ") "hello world"))
  (test all_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "\t\n\r   ") ""))
  (test mixed_whitespace (assert-eq (trim_whitespace "\t leading \n and trailing \r ") "leading \n and trailing")))

(module BulkPricing
  ;; Helper to calculate the base cost before any discounts.
  (defun calculate_subtotal ((price : Num) (qty : Num)) : Num
    (return (* price qty)))

  ;; Helper to determine if a purchase qualifies for a bulk discount.
  (defun is_bulk ((qty : Num) (threshold : Num)) : Bool
    (return (>= qty threshold)))

  ;; Helper to apply a percentage discount to an amount.
  (defun apply_discount ((amount : Num) (pct : Num)) : Num
    (return (- amount (/ (* amount pct) 100))))

  ;; Helper to round a number to two decimal places.
  (defun round2 ((x : Num)) : Num
    (return (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100)))

  ;; Calculates the final price for an item, applying a bulk discount if the quantity meets the threshold.
  ;; base_price: The price of a single item.
  ;; quantity: The number of items being purchased.
  ;; discount_threshold: The minimum quantity required to receive a bulk discount.
  ;; discount_percentage: The percentage discount applied if the threshold is met.
  (defun calculate_final_price ((base_price : Num) (quantity : Num) (discount_threshold : Num) (discount_percentage : Num)) : Num
    (def subtotal : Num (calculate_subtotal base_price quantity))
    (def final_price : Num
      (if (is_bulk quantity discount_threshold)
        (apply_discount subtotal discount_percentage)
        subtotal))
    (return (round2 final_price)))

  ;; considering the first 60 minutes of parking are free.

Implement a function that calculates the length of a given string without using the built-in `str.length` function. The length should be determined by iteratively checking for the presence of characters.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module StringLengthNoBuiltin
  (defun calculate_string_length ((s : Str)) : Num
    (def i : Num 0)
    (def current_char : Str (str.substring s i 1))
    (while (str.eq current_char "") (do
      (return 0)))
    (while (not (str.eq current_char "")) (do
      (def i : Num (+ i 1))
      (def current_char : Str (str.substring s i 1))))
    (return i))

(module ShippingCostCalculator
  (defun get_zone_base_rate ((zone : Str)) : Num
    (if (str.eq zone "ZoneA")
      (return 5.0)
      (if (str.eq zone "ZoneB")
        (return 8.5)
        (if (str.eq zone "ZoneC")
          (return 12.0)
          ; Default base rate for any other zone
          (return 15.0)))))

  (defun get_weight_surcharge ((weight : Num)) : Num
    (if (<= weight 1.0)
      (return 0.0)
      (if (<= weight 5.0)
        (return 2.5)
        (if (<= weight 10.0)
          (return 7.0)
          ; Surcharge for weights over 10.0
          (return 15.0)))))

  (defun calculate_shipping_cost ((zone : Str) (weight : Num)) : Num
    (def base_rate : Num (get_zone_base_rate zone))
    (def surcharge : Num (get_weight_surcharge weight))
    (return (+ base_rate surcharge)))

  (test zone_c_heavy_weight

(module IntegerPower

    (if (= exponent 0) (return 1) 0)
    (def res : Num 1)
    (def i : Num 0)
    (while (< i exponent) (do
      (def res : Num (* res base))
      (def i : Num (+ i 1))))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Develop a set of functions to classify a person's current heart rate into standard training zones based on their age-predicted maximum heart rate. The system should take a current heart rate and age, then return a string indicating the appropriate heart rate zone.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HeartRateClassifier
  (defun calculate_mhr ((age : Num)) : Num
    "Calculates the age-predicted maximum heart rate (MHR) using the formula 220 - age."
    (return (- 220 age)))

  (defun get_percentage_of_mhr ((current_hr : Num) (mhr : Num)) : Num
    "Calculates the current heart rate as a percentage of the maximum heart rate."
    (return (/ (* current_hr 100) mhr)))

  (defun classify_hr_zone ((current_hr : Num) (age : Num)) : Str
    "Classifies the current heart rate into one of five standard training zones or 'Below Zone 1'."
    (def mhr : Num (calculate_mhr age))
    (def percentage : Num (get_percentage_of_mhr current_hr mhr))
    (if (>= percentage 90)
      (return "Zone 5: Maximum") ; 90-100% of MHR
      (if (>= percentage 80)
        (return "Zone 4: Hard")    ; 80-89% of MHR
        (if (>= percentage 70)
          (return "Zone 3: Moderate") ; 70-79% of MHR
          (if (>= percentage 60)
            (return "Zone 2: Light")    ; 60-69% of MHR
            (if (>= percentage 50)
              (return "Zone 1: Very Light") ; 50-59% of MHR
              (return "Below Zone 1"))))))) ; Below 50% of MHR

  (test zone5_young (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 180 30) "Zone 5: Maximum")) ; MHR=190, 180/190 = 94.7%
  (test zone4_young (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 160 30) "Zone 4: Hard"))    ; MHR=190, 160/190 = 84.2%
  (test zone3_middle (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 126 50) "Zone 3: Moderate")) ; MHR=170, 126/170 = 74.1%
  (test zone2_old (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 96 60) "Zone 2: Light"))      ; MHR=160, 96/160 = 60.0%
  (test zone1_old (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 80 70) "Zone 1: Very Light"))  ; MHR=150, 80/150 = 53.3%
  (test below_zone1 (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 70 30) "Below Zone 1"))     ; MHR=190, 70/190 = 36.8%
  (test edge_zone3_to_2 (assert-eq (classify_hr_zone 119 30) "Zone 2: Light"))) ; MHR=190, 119/190 = 62.6%
